title: Trouble Diagnosis — Stall Test

figures_detected.description: Illustration showing engine and N·CVT oil level check
Illustration showing vehicle with parking brake set and wheels blocked
Illustration showing selector lever moved to D range with brake pedal pressed
Illustration showing driver pressing accelerator pedal to wide open throttle while pressing foot brake
Illustration showing selector lever moved to N range

steps.text: Check the oil level in engine and N·CVT. Replenish if necessary.
Drive the vehicle approximately 10 minutes to warm the engine and N·CVT oil to 50 to 80°C.
Set the parking brake and block the wheels securely.
Start the engine, press the brake pedal and move the selector lever to the "D" range.
While pressing the foot brake, depress the accelerator pedal gradually until it is wide open.
Quickly read the engine stall speed. Remove your foot from the accelerator pedal immediately.
notes: Do not open the throttle valve for more than 5 seconds during the test.

steps.text: Move the select lever to the "N" range.
Allow the N·CVT fluid to cool off. Run the engine at idle speed at least one minute before performing the stall test again.
notes: Refer to diagnosis chart on following page

items.label: Stall speed
items.condition: Selector lever in D range, wide open throttle, foot brake applied

model_notes: Procedure applies to N·CVT (Nissan Continuously Variable Transmission) equipped vehicles.
